name: avatar
title: Avatar
description: A person or organization identity primitive with resilient image fallback, optional non-color-only status, sizes, and RTL-safe groups.
useWhen: Showing a compact visual identity beside a person's or organization's visible name
Representing several participants in a bounded group with an explicit overflow count
Showing presence or another status when the badge also has a localized text alternative
avoidWhen: The person's name or role is more important than their image — always keep useful visible text nearby
A status would be communicated by badge color alone; provide AvatarBadge label or visible status text
Using the avatar itself as an unlabeled control; wrap it in a named Button with an adequate target
Loading a remote profile image without an appropriate privacy, retention, and failure strategy

keyboard: Avatar is non-interactive and adds no tab stop or keyboard behavior
Interactive avatar controls must be wrapped in a named control with the Commons target-size contract
Image alt text is required by the TypeScript API; use an empty alt when adjacent visible text already names the person
A meaningful badge requires a localized label so status is not conveyed by color alone
Group overlap and badge placement use logical properties and retain meaning in RTL
accessibility.targetSize: Not applicable until placed inside an interactive control
